topic.name: Cricket Players and Careers
topic.description: The documents discuss the careers and achievements of various cricketers from England and Australia.

Document 1: Jos Buttler is an English cricketer who plays for Lancashire County Cricket Club and England.
He is a right-handed batsman who also plays as a wicket-keeper.
Document 2: Doug Ring was an Australian cricketer who played for Victoria and Australia in 13 Tests.
He took 426 wickets bowling leg spin in 129 first-class matches.
Document 3: Geoffrey Boycott is a former Yorkshire and England cricketer.
He established himself as one of England's most successful opening batsmen.
Document 4: Ray Harvey is a former Australian first-class cricketer who played for Victoria.
He was the brother of Australian Test batsmen Merv and Neil.
Document 5: Arnold Fothergill was an English professional cricketer who played first-class cricket for Somerset.
He appeared for England in two Test matches in 1889.
Document 6: Joe Darling was an Australian cricketer who played 34 Test matches as a specialist batsman.
He was captain of the Australian cricket team in England in 1902.
Document 7: Arthur Morris was a key member of Donald Bradman's famous Australian cricket team.
He played in all five Tests during the 1948 tour of England.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling Events and Personalities
topic.description: This cluster focuses on significant professional wrestling events, key figures, and their contributions within major wrestling promotions like WWE and TNA.

Document 1: Survivor Series (2004) was the 18th annual Survivor Series professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The predominant match on the SmackDown! brand was John 'Bradshaw' Layfield (JBL) versus Booker T for the WWE Championship.
Document 2: Terri Runnels is a retired professional wrestling manager, television host, and occasional professional wrestler.
Runnels began her professional wrestling career in World Championship Wrestling (WCW) as Miss Alexandra York.
Document 3: Victory Road (2004) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA).
The main event was a Ladder match for the NWA World Heavyweight Championship.
Document 4: John Randall Hennigan is an American professional wrestler and actor, best known for his tenure with the WWE.
Hennigan entered Tough Enough III, a televised competition that would award the winner a WWE contract.
Document 5: Michelle Leigh McCool-Calaway is an American retired professional wrestler who is best known for her time with WWE.
She joined WWE in 2004 after participating in the 2004 WWE Diva Search.
Document 6: No Surrender (2008) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The main event was a Three Ways to Glory match for the TNA World Heavyweight Championship.
Document 7: Michael Shawn Hickenbottom, better known by his ring name Shawn Michaels, is an American professional wrestling personality.
Michaels wrestled consistently for WWE, formerly the World Wrestling Federation (WWF), from 1988 until his first retirement in 1998.

topic.name: Literary Figures and Their Works
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable literary figures and their contributions to literature, including their personal backgrounds and the impact of their works.

Document 1: Richard Henry Savage was an American military officer and author who wrote more than 40 books of adventure and mystery.
Savage's eloquent, witty, dashing and daring life may have been the inspiration for the pulp novel character Doc Savage.
Document 2: Bruce Chatwin was an English travel writer, novelist, and journalist.
His first book, In Patagonia, established Chatwin as a travel writer, although he considered himself instead a storyteller.
Document 4: Billy DeBeck was an American cartoonist, most famous as the creator of the comic strip Barney Google.
The strip was especially popular in the 1920s and 1930s, and featured a number of well-known characters.
Document 5: Allegra Byron was the illegitimate daughter of the poet George Gordon, Lord Byron.
She lived most of her short life with boarders chosen by Byron or in a Roman Catholic convent.
Document 6: Anna Wintour is a British-American journalist and editor, editor-in-chief of Vogue since 1988.
Her career in fashion journalism began at two British magazines.

topic.name: American Basketball Players
topic.description: The documents discuss various American basketball players, their careers, achievements, and contributions to the sport.

Document 1: Tim Frazier is an American professional basketball player for the New Orleans Pelicans of the National Basketball Association (NBA).
He earned various accolades with Penn State, including first-team All-Big Ten.
Document 2: Manny Harris is an American professional basketball player for the Anhui Wenyi of the Chinese NBL.
He is a former All-Big Ten Conference guard who played three seasons for the Michigan Wolverines.

Carlsen reached his peak rating of 2882, which is the highest in history.

His 100 m personal best of 9.69 seconds is the American record and makes him tied for second fastest athlete ever.

He has been ranked continuously inside the top 20 since August 2012.

Document 7: Paavo Nurmi was a Finnish middle- and long-distance runner, nicknamed the 'Flying Finn'.
He won nine gold and three silver medals in his twelve events in the Olympic Games.

topic.name: State Highways in the U.S.
topic.description: This topic covers various state highways across the United States, detailing their routes, history, and significance.

Document 1: Mississippi Highway 444 is a short highway in western Mississippi.
MS 444 was designated in 1958, and became paved two years later.
Document 2: State Route 257 is a 26.19-mile north-south state highway in Ohio.
SR 257 was created in 1926, and extended in 1935 and 1937.
Document 3: State Route 174 is a state highway in California.
The highway connects Interstate 80 in Colfax with SR 20 / SR 49 in Grass Valley.
Document 4: M-218 was a state trunkline highway in Michigan.
M-218 was originally designated by 1936 and extended into Pontiac in 1938.
Document 5: Delaware Route 44 is a state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
The route was built as a state highway east of Hartly by 1924.
Document 6: State Route 401 is a 12.13-mile long state highway in Washington.
The roadway travels northeast, paralleling cliffs, the Columbia River and the South Fork of the Naselle River.
Document 7: State Route 243 is a 30-mile two-lane highway in California.
The highway is a connector between Interstate 10 and SR 74.

topic.name: Battleship History and Design
topic.description: This topic covers the history, design, and operational roles of various classes of battleships from different navies, primarily during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

Document 1: Gneisenau was a German capital ship, alternatively described as a battleship and battlecruiser, of Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine.
Gneisenau and Scharnhorst operated together for much of the early portion of World War II.
Document 2: The Borodino-class battleship was a class of five pre-dreadnought battleships built for the Imperial Russian Navy around the end of the 19th century.
Three of these ships were sunk and one was captured by the Imperial Japanese Navy at the Battle of Tsushima in 1905.
Document 3: The High Seas Fleet was the battle fleet of the German Imperial Navy and saw action during the First World War.
The primary component of the Fleet was its battleships, typically organized in eight-ship squadrons.
Document 4: The Swiftsure class was a group of two British pre-dreadnought battleships.
Both ships rejoined Home Fleet in 1912 and were transferred abroad in 1913.
Document 5: The Petropavlovsk class was a class of three pre-dreadnought battleships built for the Imperial Russian Navy during the 1890s.
All three ships participated in the Battle of Port Arthur on the second day of the war.
Document 6: HMS Implacable was an Implacable-class aircraft carrier built for the Royal Navy during World War II.
The Implacable class had its origin as an improved version of the Illustrious-class aircraft carriers for the 1938 Naval Programme.
Document 7: Sissoi Veliky was a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the 1890s.
The ship's construction was marred by organizational, logistical and engineering problems.

topic.name: Historical Political Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss notable historical political figures and their contributions, primarily in North America.

Document 1: Robert White served as Attorney General of West Virginia and was involved in military service during the Civil War.
White devoted his efforts to bringing economic development to the South Branch Potomac River.
Document 2: Harry Strom was the ninth Premier of Alberta, Canada, and is remembered for his honest but politically ineffective leadership.
Strom's tenure marked the end of the Social Credit dynasty in Alberta.
Document 3: Jane McCrea's slaying during the American Revolutionary War became a significant propaganda tool for Patriot recruiting.
Her story entered American folklore and was referenced in literary works.
Document 4: Thomas Hinde was Northern Kentucky's first physician and played a critical role during the American Revolutionary War.
Hinde's descendants became prominent historical figures.
Document 5: John Robert Boyle served as a Member of the Legislative Assembly of Alberta and a judge on the Supreme Court of Alberta.
Boyle was involved in significant political events, including the Alberta and Great Waterways Railway scandal.
Document 6: The Old Court – New Court controversy involved a political struggle over the Kentucky Court of Appeals in the 19th century.
The controversy was rooted in economic distress and debt relief movements in Kentucky.
Document 7: Peter Dawson was a Canadian minister and politician who served in the Legislative Assembly of Alberta.
Dawson was appointed Speaker of the Legislative Assembly and had a long political career.

topic.name: Shark and Marine Species
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of sharks and their ecological roles, alongside a few other aquatic species.

Document 1: The great white shark (Carcharodon carcharias) is a species of large lamniform shark which can be found in the coastal surface waters of all the major oceans.
The great white shark is arguably the world's largest known extant macropredatory fish.
Document 2: The Caribbean reef shark (Carcharhinus perezii) is a species of requiem shark, belonging to the family Carcharhinidae.
It is one of the largest apex predators in the reef ecosystem.
Document 6: The copper shark, bronze whaler, or narrowtooth shark (Carcharhinus brachyurus) is a species of requiem shark.
Feeding mainly on cephalopods, bony fishes, and other cartilaginous fishes, the copper shark is a fast-swimming predator.

topic.name: Georgia Tech History and Traditions
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical development, significant events, and enduring traditions of the Georgia Institute of Technology.

Document 1: Founded on October 13, 1885 in Atlanta, Georgia as the Georgia School of Technology.
In 1948, the name was changed to the Georgia Institute of Technology to reflect its evolution from an engineering school to a full technical institute and research university.

One especially well-known tradition that has existed nearly since the school's establishment is Clean, Old-Fashioned Hate, Georgia Tech's heated, long-standing and ongoing rivalry with the University of Georgia.

topic.name: Marine Biology and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various species of sharks and their ecological significance, alongside conservation efforts and biological characteristics.

Document 1: The hardnose shark is fished for meat throughout its range and, given its low reproductive rate, the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has listed it as Near Threatened.

Document 4: The dark shyshark curls into a ring with its tail covering its eyes, hence the name 'shyshark'.
The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has listed the dark shyshark under Least Concern.
Document 5: The blotchy swellshark is known to consume numerous types of fishes and invertebrates, including an unusually high diversity of cartilaginous fishes.
It is caught as bycatch in commercial bottom fishing.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Their Impact
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable historical figures and their contributions to society, politics, and culture across different regions and eras.

Document 1: Roberto Cofresí, better known as El Pirata Cofresí, was a pirate from Puerto Rico.
Cofresí began his new career in early 1823, filling a role vacant in the Spanish Main since the death of Jean Lafitte.
Document 2: The Han dynasty was an age of great economic, technological, cultural, and social progress in China.
Its society was governed by an emperor who shared power with an official bureaucracy and semi-feudal nobility.
Document 3: Constantine was the son of Flavius Valerius Constantius, a Roman army officer.
As emperor, Constantine enacted many administrative, financial, social, and military reforms to strengthen the empire.
Document 4: Kiliaen van Rensselaer was a Dutch diamond and pearl merchant from Amsterdam who was one of the founders and directors of the Dutch West India Company.
He founded the Manor of Rensselaerswyck in what is now mainly New York's Capital District.
Document 5: Thomas Spottswood Hinde was an American newspaper editor, opponent of slavery, author, historian, and a founder of the city of Mount Carmel, Illinois.
Hinde was an active businessman, pursuing real estate, construction, and publishing opportunities in Kentucky, Ohio and Illinois.
Document 6: The Madras Presidency was an administrative subdivision of British India.
The city of Madras was the winter capital of the Presidency and Ootacamund or Ooty, the summer capital.
Document 7: Ethan Allen is best known as one of the founders of the U.S. state of Vermont.
Allen led the Green Mountain Boys in a campaign of intimidation and property destruction to drive New York settlers from the Grants.

topic.name: Olympic and Paralympic Athletes
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the achievements and biographies of athletes who have competed in the Olympic and Paralympic Games.

1: Britt Dillmann is a wheelchair basketball forward who won a silver medal at the 1988 Summer Paralympics.
She staged a comeback in 2011, winning a gold medal at the 2012 Summer Paralympics.
2: Kelsey Wakefield is an Australian water polo player who has been a member of the Australia women's national water polo team and was selected for the preliminary Australian water polo team for the 2012 Summer Olympics.
3: Barbados sent a delegation to compete at the 2008 Summer Olympics in Beijing, marking its tenth appearance as an independent nation.
The nation's flagbearer during the Beijing Games was swimmer Bradley Ally.
4: Catie Ball won a gold medal at the 1968 Summer Olympics as a member of the winning U.S. 4 × 100-meter medley relay team.
5: Rebecca Soni is a six-time Olympic medalist who won gold at the 2008 Summer Olympics and successfully defended her title at the 2012 Summer Olympics.
6: Meica Christensen was selected to represent Australia at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in goalball.
7: Feng Tianwei represented Singapore at the 2008 Summer Olympics, winning a silver medal in the women's team event.

topic.name: Naval Warfare in World Wars

Nürnberg served with the fleet briefly, before being deployed overseas in 1910.

Beautiful Eyes peaked at number nine on the U.S. Billboard 200 and topped Billboard's Top Country Albums chart.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclone seasons, detailing the formation, impact, and statistics of storms in both the Atlantic and Pacific regions.

Document 1: The 1961 Atlantic hurricane season featured the highest number of major hurricanes – Category 3 or higher
It was an above average season in terms of tropical storms, with a total of 11 named storms.
Document 2: The 1940 Pacific typhoon season marked an interruption in meteorological records due to the start of World War II
There were 43 reported tropical cyclone, including 27 that attained typhoon status.
Document 3: The 1939 Pacific typhoon season has no official bounds; it ran year-round in 1939
There were 29 known tropical cyclones, including 24 of typhoon status.
Document 4: The 2012 Atlantic hurricane season was extremely active, tied with 1887, 1995, 2010, and 2011 for having the third-most named storms on record
The season officially began on June 1 and ended on November 30.
Document 5: The 1956 Atlantic hurricane season featured a low number of tropical cyclones, although every tropical storm and hurricane affected land
There were twelve tropical storms, a third of which became hurricanes.
Document 6: The 1989 – 90 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was an average cyclone season, with nine named storms
The first storm, Cyclone Alibera, was the second longest-lasting tropical cyclone on record in the basin.
Document 7: The 1990 – 91 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was fairly quiet, although activity began early
Cyclone Bella became the strongest storm of the season, reaching 10 ‑ minute maximum sustained winds of 155 km/h.

topic.name: Waterways and Tropical Cyclones
topic.description: This cluster discusses various waterways and the impacts of tropical cyclones in the United States.

Document 1: Darby Creek is a tributary of the Delaware River in Pennsylvania.
The watershed of the creek has an area of 77.2 square miles.
Document 2: Hurricane Igor was the most destructive tropical cyclone to strike Newfoundland.
Igor reached Category 4 status on the Saffir – Simpson Hurricane Wind Scale.
Document 3: The Potlatch River is a major tributary to the Clearwater River.
The ecology of the river is still in the process of recovery.
Document 4: Fossil Creek is a perennial stream in Arizona.
Fossil Creek is one of only two streams in Arizona included in the National Wild and Scenic Rivers System.
Document 5: Tropical cyclone observation has been carried out over the past couple of centuries.
Advances in technology have included using planes to survey the ocean basins.
Document 6: Hurricane Gordon caused minor damage in the Eastern United States.
Gordon developed in the extreme western Caribbean Sea from a tropical wave.
Document 7: Big Wapwallopen Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in Pennsylvania.
The creek is designated as a Coldwater Fishery and a Migratory Fishery.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Their Impact
topic.description: The documents discuss significant historical figures and their contributions to society, politics, and culture in various contexts.

Document 1: Marie-Rosalie Cadron-Jetté was a Canadian widow and midwife who undertook the charitable care of unwed and struggling mothers.

Document 2: Klaus Fuchs was a German theoretical physicist and atomic spy who was convicted of supplying information to the Soviet Union.

Document 3: Robert Latham Owen was one of the first two U.S. senators from Oklahoma.

topic.name: Medieval European Leadership
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical figures and events related to the governance, conflicts, and notable leadership in medieval Europe.

Document 6: Jeremi Wiśniowiecki was a notable member of the aristocracy of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth.

topic.name: Formula One and NASCAR Racing
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history, events, and notable figures in both Formula One and NASCAR racing, highlighting significant races, championships, and drivers.

1: The 2008 Turkish Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 11 May 2008 at the Istanbul Racing Circuit.
Felipe Massa won the race for the Ferrari team after starting from pole position.
2: Mario Andretti is an Italian American former racing driver, one of the most successful Americans in the history of the sport.
He is one of only two drivers to win races in Formula One, IndyCar, World Sportscar Championship and NASCAR.
3: The 2011 Daytona 500 was held on February 20, 2011 at Daytona International Speedway as the first race of the 2011 NASCAR Sprint Cup season.

4: The 2013 Mudsummer Classic was a NASCAR Camping World Truck Series stock car race held on July 24, 2013.
It was the first dirt track race held by a NASCAR national touring series since 1970.
5: The 1997 European Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 26 October 1997 at the Circuito Permanente de Jerez, Spain.
Mika Häkkinen won the race in a McLaren, his first Formula One race victory.
6: The 2008 Spanish Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 27 April 2008 at the Circuit de Catalunya, Barcelona, Spain.
Kimi Räikkönen won the race for the Ferrari team after starting from pole position.
7: The 2015 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held at the Yas Marina Circuit on 29 November 2015.
Nico Rosberg secured his sixth consecutive pole position and went on to win the race.

topic.name: Notable Buildings and Structures

It was the tallest building in Perth from its completion in 1988 until 1992.

topic.name: U.S. and State Highways
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. and state highways, detailing their routes, historical significance, and geographical connections.

1: Maryland Route 355 is a 36.75-mile north-south road in western central Maryland.
MD 355 serves as a major thoroughfare through Frederick and Montgomery counties.
2: State Route 20 is a state highway in the northern central region of California, running east-west across the state.
All of SR 20 is on the Interregional Road System, a highway system that connects major economic centers of the state.
3: U.S. Highway 136 is a short U.S. Highway in Keokuk, Iowa.
The highway was designated nationally in 1951 and has remained largely unchanged through Keokuk since then.
4: U.S. Route 15 runs from Walterboro, South Carolina to Painted Post, New York.
US 15 is the primary north-south highway of Frederick County.
5: King's Highway 48 is a provincially maintained highway in southern Ontario.
Highway 48 was first designated in 1937 to connect Port Bolster with Highway 12 in Beaverton.
6: Sierra Highway or El Camino Sierra is a road in Southern California, United States.
El Camino Sierra connects Los Angeles with Lake Tahoe along the eastern edge of California.
7: State Route 21 is a 191.34-mile long state highway in Washington.
Since 1899, at least one segment of the current highway has been in the state highway system.

topic.name: Mathematical and Computational Concepts
topic.description: This topic encompasses various mathematical and computational theories and algorithms used in fields such as computer graphics, graph theory, physics, and electrical engineering.

Document 1: Fast inverse square root is a method of calculating the reciprocal of a square root for a 32-bit floating point number.
Inverse square roots are used to compute angles of incidence and reflection for lighting and shading in computer graphics.
Document 2: In graph theory, a book embedding is a generalization of planar embedding of a graph to embeddings into a book.
The book thickness of a graph is the smallest possible number of half-planes for any book embedding of the graph.
Document 3: A Hilbert space extends the methods of vector algebra and calculus from the two-dimensional Euclidean plane and three-dimensional space to spaces with any finite or infinite number of dimensions.
Hilbert spaces arise naturally and frequently in mathematics and physics.
Document 4: Negative resistance is a property of some electrical circuits and devices in which an increase in voltage results in a decrease in electric current.
Negative resistance can amplify an electrical signal, converting DC power applied to its terminals to AC output power.
Document 6: The number e is an important mathematical constant that is the base of the natural logarithm.
The function f(x) = e^x is called the exponential function, and its inverse is the natural logarithm.
Document 7: The impedance analogy is a method of representing a mechanical system by an analogous electrical system.
The mathematical behaviour of the simulated electrical system is identical to the mathematical behaviour of the represented mechanical system.

topic.name: Cultural Reflections and Innovations
topic.description: The documents explore various cultural narratives and innovations in literature, cuisine, maritime history, and personal essays.

Document 4: Even the Stars Look Lonesome is African-American writer and poet Maya Angelou 's second book of essays

topic.name: Naval Warships of the Late 19th Century
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of naval warships built during the late 19th century, focusing on their design, armament, and service history.

Document 1: Caprera was a torpedo cruiser of the Partenope class built for the Italian Regia Marina in the 1880s.
She served in the Red Sea during the Italo-Turkish War of 1911 – 12.
Document 2: The Hector-class ironclads were a pair of armoured frigates built for the Royal Navy in the 1860s.
They were mobilized during the Russo-Turkish War of 1877 – 78, but saw no action.
Document 3: Masséna was a pre-dreadnought battleship of the French Navy, built in the 1890s.
She served in both the Northern and Mediterranean Squadrons during her career.
Document 4: The Ammiraglio di Saint Bon class was a pair of pre-dreadnought battleships built for the Italian Navy during the 1890s.
They participated in the Italo-Turkish War of 1911 – 12.
Document 5: The Satsuma class was a pair of semi-dreadnought battleships built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the first decade of the 20th century.
They marked a transitional stage between the pre-dreadnought and true dreadnought designs.
Document 6: HMS Zealous was one of the three ships forming the second group of wooden steam battleships selected in 1860 for conversion to ironclads.
She became the flagship for the Pacific Station for six years until she was relieved in 1872.
Document 7: SMS Zieten was the first aviso built for the Imperial German Navy.
She was the fastest ship in the German fleet at the time.

topic.name: North American Fungi
topic.description: This topic encompasses various species of fungi found in North America, focusing on their characteristics, taxonomy, and habitats.

Document 1: Wynnea americana is a species of fungus in the family Sarcoscyphaceae found in eastern North America.
It is found from New York to Michigan south to Mexico.
Document 2: Hygrophorus bakerensis is a species of fungus in the Hygrophoraceae family known from the United States.
It is common in coniferous forests throughout the Pacific Northwest.
Document 3: Strobilomyces foveatus is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family first reported from Malaysia and Australia.
Fruit bodies are characterized by the small dark brown to black conical scales covering the cap.
Document 4: Lactifluus deceptivus is a common species of fungus in the Russulaceae family found throughout eastern North America.
It produces large mushrooms with funnel-shaped caps in coniferous and deciduous forests.
Document 5: Stropharia caerulea is an inedible species of agaric fungus found in Europe and North America.
It grows as a saprophyte in meadows, roadsides, and gardens.
Document 6: Macrolepiota clelandii is a species of mushroom-forming fungus found in Australia and New Zealand.
It fruits in eucalypt woodlands, parks, and roadsides.
Document 7: Sutorius eximius is a species of fungus in the family Boletaceae widely distributed in North America, South America, and Asia.
It grows in a mycorrhizal relationship with both coniferous and deciduous trees.

topic.name: Amusement Park Attractions

Document 2: Thunder Run is a wooden roller coaster at the Kentucky Kingdom amusement park in Louisville, Kentucky.

topic.name: Japanese Video Game Culture
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of Japanese video games, including their development, localization, and cultural impact.

1: Air is a Japanese adult visual novel developed by Key released on September 8, 2000 for Windows PCs.

7: Symphonic Odysseys: Tribute to Nobuo Uematsu was a symphonic tribute concert held in Cologne, Germany on July 9, 2011.

topic.name: Historical Engineering Structures
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant historical engineering structures, including castles and dams, highlighting their architectural, functional, and cultural importance across different periods and regions.

Document 1: Conwy Castle is a medieval fortification in Conwy, on the north coast of Wales.
The castle played an important part in several wars.

It continues to be fully functional for hydroelectric generation.
Document 3: The Stainforth and Keadby Canal is a navigable canal in South Yorkshire and Lincolnshire, England.
It became part of the Sheffield and South Yorkshire Navigation.

topic.name: Atlantic Hurricane History
topic.description: A historical overview of significant tropical storms and hurricanes that have impacted the Atlantic region, detailing their formation, intensity, and effects on land.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Alpha was the twenty-third named storm of the 2005 Atlantic hurricane season.
It caused 26 deaths, 17 of them in Haiti and all of them caused by floods and rain-related landslides.
Document 2: Hurricane Helene was the ninth tropical storm, fourth hurricane, and strongest hurricane of the 2006 Atlantic hurricane season.
It never affected land until the very end of its lifespan as a weak extratropical system.
Document 3: The 1948 Miami hurricane caused no fatalities in Florida, despite moving across the Miami area as a hurricane.
The storm intensified into a tropical storm early the next day and a hurricane several hours later.
Document 4: The 1940 New England hurricane produced strong winds and torrential rainfall.
The hurricane continued to intensify, and reached peak intensity as a Category 2 hurricane.
Document 5: Hurricane Madeline was a Category 4 hurricane that made landfall in Mexico in October 1976.
Heavy damage was reported, along with seven fatalities.
Document 6: The September 1948 Florida hurricane was the most intense tropical cyclone to make landfall in the state since the 1935 Labor Day hurricane.
Severe destruction was reported on the island, with winds up to 90 mph observed in Havana.
Document 7: Hurricane Ella was the second of two major hurricanes to threaten Texas during the 1970 Atlantic hurricane season.
It developed on September 8 in the western Caribbean Sea.
